# UC-11.3.65 · Proofpoint URL Defense User Click Risk (Allowed vs Blocked)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.*

---

## Description

For malicious URLs, comparing permitted versus blocked clicks shows users who bypassed browser protections or policy exceptions—prime candidates for containment and training.

## Value

Separates noisy total click volume from high-risk permitted clicks on known-bad URLs, closing the gap between gateway detection and endpoint compromise.

## Implementation

Map `userPrincipalName` from your click feed—some deployments use `user` or `recipient`. Align `action` vocabulary (`allowed`, `permitted`, `not blocked`). Integrate with IdP username fields for password resets. Respect privacy policies on URL storage.

## SPL

```spl
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:click" earliest=-24h
| eval verdict=lower(coalesce(verdict, Verdict, ""))
| eval action=lower(coalesce(action, Action, disposition, ""))
| where verdict="malicious"
| stats count(eval(action="allowed" OR action="permitted")) as permitted_clicks count as total_clicks by userPrincipalName, url
| eval permit_rate=round(100*permitted_clicks/nullif(total_clicks,0),1)
| where permitted_clicks>0
| sort -permitted_clicks
```

## Visualization

Table (user × URL × permitted count), bar chart (top users), timeline.

## Known False Positives

Training clicks, helpdesk rewrites, and sandbox detonation that still show as user follow-through in logs.

## References

- [Proofpoint URL Defense](https://www.proofpoint.com/us/products/advanced-threat-protection)
- [Proofpoint TAP Modular Input for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3681)
